raman drug detection.overview: Raman Drug Detection is a rapid screening project that uses Raman spectroscopy and machine learning to identify suspect pharmaceutical samples and potential counterfeit products. It is designed for fast triage: identify likely matches, flag anomalies, and route uncertain cases for confirmatory lab testing.
raman drug detection.problem_statement: Field teams need a fast, consistent way to screen medicines where packaging and labels can be misleading. Traditional lab methods are accurate but slow and expensive. The goal is to reduce time-to-decision while preserving traceability and quality controls.
architecture: Ingestion service reads raw spectra from devices or uploads.
Pre-processing pipeline performs baseline correction, smoothing, normalization.
Feature extraction generates engineered spectral features and/or embeddings.
Classification service predicts candidate compounds and confidence.
Review UI shows spectrum, prediction, and audit trail.
Storage keeps raw spectra, processed spectra, model versions, and decisions.

workflow.title: Capture spectrum
workflow.details: Collect Raman spectrum from the handheld device or bench instrument.

workflow.title: Pre-process spectrum
workflow.details: Apply baseline correction, denoising, and normalization.

workflow.title: Match against reference library
workflow.details: Compute similarity against curated reference spectra and known classes.

workflow.title: Human review (if needed)
workflow.details: Analysts review low-confidence cases and add notes/labels.

workflow.title: Decision + reporting
workflow.details: Generate a screening report with model version, evidence, and final decision.

challenges: Class imbalance and limited labeled data for rare compounds
Maintaining traceability across model versions and reference libraries
use_cases: Rapid triage before confirmatory lab testing

triples.o: Overview
Problem Statement
1. Ingestion service reads raw spectra from devices or uploads.
2. Pre-processing pipeline performs baseline correction, smoothing, normalization.
3. Feature extraction generates engineered spectral features and/or embeddings.
4. Classification service predicts candidate compounds and confidence.
5. Review UI shows spectrum, prediction, and audit trail.
6. Storage keeps raw spectra, processed spectra, model versions, and decisions.
Step 1: Capture spectrum — Collect Raman spectrum from the handheld device or bench instrument.
Step 2: Pre-process spectrum — Apply baseline correction, denoising, and normalization.
Step 3: Match against reference library — Compute similarity against curated reference spectra and known classes.
Step 4: Human review (if needed) — Analysts review low-confidence cases and add notes/labels.
Step 5: Decision + reporting — Generate a screening report with model version, evidence, and final decision.
